title: Case geometry
caption: Impinging-shock SWBLI configuration: forebody-ramp shock generator, internal cowl wall, incident and reflected shocks.

title: Computational mesh
caption: Wall-normal-clustered structured grid (y+≈0.7) with Ducros-sensor adaptive refinement banded around the captured shock.

title: Convergence history
caption: Density/energy residuals fall below 1e-6; drag and separation location reach steady values.

title: Grid independence
caption: Key metrics asymptote with mesh refinement; fine-grid GCI < 2%.

title: Wall pressure
caption: Free-interaction pressure rise to the separation plateau, then the reattachment compression to p/p∞ ≈ 5.45.

title: Skin friction
caption: Cf drops below zero inside the separation bubble and recovers to turbulent levels after reattachment.

title: Heat transfer
caption: Aerothermal load peaks at reattachment (q_w ≈ 0.9 MW/m²), the design-driving heat load for cowl structure.

title: BL thickness
caption: δ, δ* and θ growth; strong thickening through the interaction as the shear layer lifts off.

title: Shape factor
caption: H rises sharply at separation, then drops to the turbulent value after transition/reattachment.

title: Transition prediction
caption: The shock-bypass source drives N across N_crit at the interaction, setting the intermittency ramp 0→1 (shock-induced transition).

title: Edge conditions
caption: Edge Mach drops 4.0→2.74 and edge temperature rises across the two-shock compression.

title: Pressure coefficient
caption: Surface Cp signature of the SWBLI.

title: Surface dashboard
caption: Overview of all surface predictions used for the design decision.

title: Re_theta
caption: Re_θ used by the transition correlation; jumps through the interaction.

title: Velocity profiles
caption: Laminar, separated (reversed near-wall flow, u<0) and reattached turbulent velocity profiles.

title: Temperature profiles
caption: Near-wall viscous heating peak (T→T_aw) with the cooled-wall condition T_w=470 K; the design heat-load driver.

title: Pressure contour
caption: 2-D static-pressure field; dashed lines = shocks, solid = BL edge.

title: Temperature contour
caption: Temperature field showing the hot viscous boundary layer and the post-shock temperature rise.

title: Mach contour
caption: Mach field: deceleration 4.0→2.74 through the two-shock compression; low-momentum boundary layer at the wall.

title: Velocity vectors
caption: Velocity vectors over the speed field; flow turns toward the wall behind the incident shock and realigns behind the reflected shock.

title: 3-D wall pressure
caption: Spanwise-extruded wall-pressure surface; the ridge marks the reattachment compression.

title: 3-D pressure field
caption: Pressure as a height field reveals the incident & reflected shock fronts as steep ramps.

title: 3-D temperature field
caption: Temperature height field: hot near-wall layer ridge plus post-shock temperature steps.

title: 3-D velocity vectors
caption: Velocity vectors on three spanwise planes showing the shock-induced flow deflection and near-wall retardation.

title: Intake performance
caption: Predicted total-pressure recovery, KE efficiency and mass capture, with the MIL-E-5008B reference.

title: Aerothermal load
caption: Wall heat-flux distribution; the reattachment peak sizes the thermal-protection / cooling requirement.
